turns.question: Propulsion system of Chandrayaan-3?
turns.answer: The propulsion system of the Chandrayaan-3 Propulsion Module is a Bi-Propellant Propulsion System that uses MMH (Monomethylhydrazine) and MON3 (Mixed Oxides of Nitrogen). It is equipped with a 440 N liquid apogee motor (LAM) as the main engine for orbit-raising maneuvers and lunar orbit insertion, and four 50 N thrusters to provide attitude control. The propulsion module has a propellant mass of 1696.39 kg, a dry mass of 448.62 kg (including pressurant), and a total mass of 2145.01 kg (reported as 2148 kg in some mission reports). Its primary function is to carry the lander module from the launch vehicle injection orbit to a 100 km circular polar lunar orbit.

citations.chunk_text: 4. Dry Mass 448.62 kg (including pressurant) 5. Propellant Mass 1696.39 kg 6. Total PM Mass 2145.01 kg ... 10. Propulsion System Bi-Propellant Propulsion System (MMH + MON3)

The Chandrayaan-3 propulsion module carries the spacecraft from launch injection to lunar orbit. It uses a 440 N liquid apogee motor (LAM) as the main engine for orbit-raising manoeuvres and lunar orbit insertion. The propulsion module has a total mass of 2148 kg including 1696.39 kg of propellant. Four 50 N thrusters provide attitude control.
The main function of PM is to carry the LM from launch vehicle injection till final lunar 100 km circular polar orbit and separate the LM from PM.
